headline: Charles Schwab Fires Latest Shot in ETF Fee War, Cutting Expenses to Near-Zero
summary: Charles Schwab’s  asset-management unit cut fees today on four equity index exchange-traded funds, a move that comes amid increasing investor demand for ETFs.  The operating expense ratios for  Schwab U.S. Mid-Cap ETF  and  are dropping to 0.03% from 0.04%.  The company says that 16 of Schwab Asset Management’s 24 market-cap weighted, index equity and fixed income ETFs are now offered at only three basis points (0.03%).

headline: Schwab Asset Management Reduces Fees on Four Equity Index ETFs
summary: WESTLAKE, Texas, June 11, 2026--Schwab Asset Management®, the asset management arm of The Charles Schwab Corporation and the fifth-largest provider1 of ETFs, today announced the reduction of operating expense ratios for four equity index ETFs: the Schwab U.S. Mid-Cap ETF (SCHM), Schwab U.S. Small-Cap ETF (SCHA), Schwab International Small-Cap Equity ETF (SCHC), and Schwab Emerging Markets Equity ETF (SCHE). headline: The Charles Schwab Corporation (SCHW): A Deep Value Stock to Invest in Now amid Cryptocurrency Focus
summary: The Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE:SCHW) is a deep value stock to invest in now. On June 2, The Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE:SCHW) strengthened its footprint in the cryptocurrency sector by introducing 24/7 cryptocurrency futures trading on the thinkorswim platform suite. The enhancement will enable round-the-clock trading of cryptocurrency futures for Bitcoin, Ether, Solana, and Ripple […]
